meta.title: Understanding and Using the Cyberwar Meta
meta.description:
Dave Aitel of Immunity Inc. looks at crypto policy, the future of offensive techniques and state v. non-state actor capabilities ... and he puts much of this in context with examples from the game Overwatch.

Dave says "I'm going to pick on some people, because they can take it. And because they are super wrong."

On the policy front he asserts it is being made by academia and the legal community, much like atomic, chemical and biological warfare, but in this case they know little about the field. They are not experts or practitioners. Dave gives specific examples.

The middle switches into offensive tactics and tools, along with some predictions. The parts on what a modern implant was left us wanting more info. Interesting and important.

Dave finishes off with a push back that Nation States have a huge skills advantage on non-state actors. Even the 

He pushes back on the idea that Nation States are the only players that can have a big impact, and makes the case that this big mega-group strategy




Offensive techniques and implants

Assumes that code has a built in "intent". 

What is an Overwatch "meta"

Immunity bet that worms would be the most obvious meta

Non-state actors are much more important than the governments and policy people realize and preach. The "outside" is not understood by the "inside".
